zh.Fingringhoe, Colchester Borough, Essex,: 英國英格蘭艾可分格林
zh.Great Clacton, Essex, England: 英國艾可格亞特 可拉可頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Bell於1530年出生在英國艾可時，她的父親Sir Robert Bell40歲，母親Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson29歲。她於1548年7月3日與Thomas Westbrome在英國艾可格亞特 可拉可頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1581年在英國英格蘭艾可分格林享年51歲，埋葬在英國艾可分格林。

zh-hans.Great Clacton, Essex, England: 英国艾可格亞特 可拉可頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Bell于1530年出生在英国艾可时，她的父亲Sir Robert Bell40岁，母亲Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson29岁。她于1548年7月3日与Thomas Westbrome在英国艾可格亞特 可拉可頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1581年在英国英格兰艾可分格林享年51岁，埋葬在英国艾可分格林。

nameMeanings.name: Bell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and Scottish (Lowlands): from the Middle English personal name Bell. As a man's name this is from Old French beu, bel ‘handsome’, which was also used as a nickname. As a female name it represents a short form of Isabel .

English (northern) and Scottish (Lowlands): from Middle English belle ‘bell’ (Old English belle), in various applications; most probably a metonymic occupational name for a bell ringer or bell maker, or a topographic name for someone living ‘at the bell’ (as attested by 14th-century forms such as John atte Belle). This indicates either residence by an actual bell (e.g. a town's bell in a bell tower, centrally placed to summon meetings, sound the alarm, etc.) or ‘at the sign of the bell’, i.e. a house or inn sign (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in Scots and English).
English: from Middle English bel ‘fair, fine, good’ (Old French bel ‘beautiful, fair’). See also Beal 1.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfonso, Alvarado, Augusto, Belia, Josefina, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Romero, Santa.

French, Spanish, Portuguese, and English (of French origin): from the Old French female personal name Isabel, which is first recorded in late 11th-century Paris and Maine (France). It was an alternative form of Elizabeth , Old French Elisabet, which had a shortened pet form Isabet. Isabel appears to have originated as a variant of Isabet, in which the suffix -el was substituted for -et through re-interpretation of -et as a hypocoristic diminutive. The resultant -bel was early associated with Old French bel ‘fair, beautiful’, as is evident in the French variant Isabeau. From France the name was taken to England, where it achieved considerable popularity in the late medieval period as an independent personal name alongside its rarer doublet Elizabeth. In the form Isabel it also appears in Spain and Portugal, while in Italy it took the form Isabella . The French surnames Isabel and Isabelle are particularly associated with Normandy.
History: Michel Isabel from Reux in Calvados, France, married Marie Jobidon in Château-Richer, QC, in 1673.

variants.name: Beller
meanings: English: occupational name for a bell founder or bell ringer, from Middle English beller, an agent derivative of Old English belle ‘bell’. Compare Bell 2.
German: habitational name for someone from any of several places called Bell in North Rhine-Westphalia or Bellen near Bremen.
German: nickname for a quarreller, from Middle High German bellen ‘to bicker, quarrel’.

variants.name: Bells
meanings: English: variant of Bell with genitival or excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bellman
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a bell ringer, in particular one whose duty was to make public announcements, after ringing a bell to attract attention (from Middle English bel(le)man, a term known to have been used in York and Scotland, and probably elsewhere, for a town crier). Compare Bell .
English: occupational name from Middle English beli-man ‘bellows blower’, from Middle English beli ‘bellows’. Compare Bellow .
Altered form of German   Bellmann  . This surname is also found in Sweden.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Bell was born in 1530, in Essex, England, her father, Sir Robert Bell, was 40 and her mother, Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, was 29. She married Thomas Westbrome on 3 July 1548, in Great Clacton, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1581, in Fingringhoe, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Fingringhoe, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Bell est née en 1530, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Bell, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Westbrome le 3 juillet 1548, à Great Clacton, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1581, à Fingringhoe, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Fingringhoe, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Bell 1530 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Bell 40 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson 29. Sie hat am 3. Juli 1548 in Great Clacton, Essex, England Thomas Westbrome geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1581 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Fingringhoe, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Fingringhoe, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bell è nata il nel 1530 nel Essex, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Bell, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Westbrome il 3 luglio 1548, nel Great Clacton, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1581, nel Fingringhoe, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Fingringhoe, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bellが1530年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Bellは40歳，母Elizabeth Cromwell Andersonは29歳であった。1548年7月3日にイギリスエセックスグレアト クラクトンでThomas Westbromeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1581年にイギリスイギリスエセックスフィングリングhオで51歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスフィングリングhオにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bell(이)가 1530년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Bell(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1548년 7월 3일 영국 에세스 그리트 클라크턴에서 Thomas Westbrome(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1581년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 핑그링호에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 핑그링호에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bell nasceu em 1530, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Bell, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Westbrome em 3 de julho de 1548, em Great Clacton, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1581, em Fingringhoe, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Fingringhoe, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Bell родилась в 1530 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Bell было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Westbrome 3 июля 1548 г. в местности грит клактон, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1581 г. в местности фингрингхо, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности фингрингхо, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Bell nació en 1530, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Bell, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Thomas Westbrome el 3 de julio de 1548, en Great Clacton, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1581, en Fingringhoe, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Fingringhoe, Essex, Inglaterra.
